title: thymus leucotrichus subsp. leucotrichus
intro: Thymus leucotrichus subsp. leucotrichus, also known as the white-petaled thyme, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. It is a low-growing, woody perennial with white or pink flowers, native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and antifungal activities, which make it effective in treating respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. Thymus leucotrichus subsp. leucotrichus can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to promote digestion, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system.
